mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This reflects a healthy economic environment where consumer spending fuels business expansion.
failure_condition: If the growth rate of RSAFS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits. This deterioration in economic fundamentals is expected to drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: High and persistent inflation combined with rising interest rates indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This increases the cost of capital, reduces future earnings expectations, and compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 1 month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive when both CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in a month, or the interaction term shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, strong month-over-month industrial production growth signals healthy manufacturing activity and business investment. This positive economic momentum boosts investor confidence, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 0.5% MoM does not lead to statistically significant positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during identified periods of economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts indicates weakening demand in the housing sector and a potential broader economic slowdown. This negatively impacts sectors tied to construction and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job creation, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity. This supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth above 100k MoM does not lead to statistically significant positive 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of high federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance. This increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, ultimately dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as growth expectations are lowered.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive or flat during periods when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for more than two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When overall economic growth (GDP) is strong but industrial production is declining, it signals a potential divergence between services and manufacturing sectors. This can create uncertainty about the sustainability of the expansion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investors anticipate a broader slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 2% AND INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive or show no significant negative trend when GDPC1 annual growth is strong and INDPRO is contracting.
